title: Fragrant and Crunchy Ethiopian Beans Wrap
description: A flavorful and crunchy Ethiopian-inspired dish that can be easily meal-prepped. Perfect for low-sodium diets.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine lentils, onion, garlic, cilantro, crushed red pepper flakes, cumin, paprika, and salt-free seasoning. Simmer over medium heat for 20-25 minutes or until the lentils are tender.
Add coconut oil to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Cut the double-rise bread into 1-inch slices and toast until lightly browned.
Add a slice of toasted bread to each lotus leaf wrap, followed by a spoonful of the lentil mixture, some sliced cucumber, and a tomato slice.
Optional: fold the bottom edge of the lotus leaf up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the wrap up tightly.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients and serve immediately, or refrigerate for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
Serve warm or at room temperature, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Note: Lotus leaf wraps can be found in most Asian grocery stores or online.
Note: Double-rise bread can be replaced with pita bread or naan for a different texture.
Note: If using frozen lotus leaf wraps, thaw according to package instructions before using.
